transcript.text:
Hello Everyone I am here at Hoengseong today
I heard that there is a popular makguksu (buckwheat noodle) restaurant around here
It’s right across the street. Let’s go then!
I heard that the place next door is also good
But this one is where I'm going today
Doesn't it look pretty old?
[This restaurant used to be a bus stop 30 years ago]
Hi~ Welcome~
There are 3 different types of makguksu
So they have a spicy one,
Soy sauce makgugsu? Never heard of it
They also have suyuk (boiled pork belly) and Nokdu-jeon (mung bean pancake)
Are you ready to order?
Can I just get one of each?
Thank you. Oh, It’s Kimchi!
Look how big it is

Smells pretty good
[Making noodles with Sun-memil dough (100% pure buckwheat dough)]
[Ganjang Makguksu (Soy sauce buckwheat noodles)]
[Myeongtae Bibim Makguksu (Spicy buckwheat noodles with pollack)]
[Nokdu-jeon (Mung Bean Pancake)]
[Suyuk (Korean boiled pork belly)]
Thank you~
You know, the weather's getting pretty hot these days, so I've been craving cold noodles
Let’s pour the cold broth
I asked them to take out the cucumber slices
Wow, the noodles are pretty thin. I was expecting much thicker noodles because it’s makguksu
Mix it carefully
It looks super tasty
Let me get a sip first
This is by far the best makguksu I’ve ever had. I think it’s because the noodles are made of 100% memil (buckwheat)
The noodles are thin enough for the seasoning to seep in

It’s so tasty. A few drops of sesame oil will make this even better
Thank you~
There we go
I’m super excited This can’t go wrong
It’s perfect for me Yummy!
Suyuk! It's super fresh. They just cooked it
Dip it in Ssamjang
It's amazing
Seriously.. [Ooh and Aah]
I can't deny that I'm a meat-lover
How can it be this tasty? Is it because they just cooked it?
I heard that they had to boil a new one because they ran out of charcoal
It's a bit oily, chewy, and savory
It's fantastic I should've ordered a large plate
Let's try with kimchi
Kimchi on top

It's super yummy
Let's try the soy sauce makguksu
I've never heard of soy sauce makguksu
The noodles break easily because it's 100% pure buckwheat
I gotta mix it carefully
It tastes nutty
Let's put some sesame oil in it
Radish on top of the noodles
It tastes great
Let's try Nokdu-jeon this time
It's very crispy
It's very light and bland but somehow addictive because it's also nutty
Suyuk is so yummy I need to hurry up and finish before it gets cold
It won't be as good if it's cold
This is my favorite, period
Fold it in half

It's really good
Next is the Hoe-naengmyeon
My bad, I mean the hoe-makguksu
I'm tripping. It's bibim makguksu
This is how you are supposed mix
[Bibim guksu (X) Bibim makguksu with pollock (O)
Put a slice of pollock on top
This is it
I like the bibim better than the plain one
The pollock is really tasty
It has a strong smell of chili powder, but it's not spicy at all. It's more like sweet and sour
The polick is a bomb
Put a slice of suyuk on top
I can't wait
It's amazing
It goes really well with sesame oil

I've had makguksu only a few times
It tastes a lot different from the dak(?)guksu...
Dak-guksu (chicken noodle)?.. What's wrong with me today
It tastes a lot different from the makguksu that usually comes with jokbal (pork feet)
It taste like pyongyang naengmyeon (She never tried it before tho)
The portions here are big
To be honest, Hoengseong is well known for beef
Now I'm craving beef
(What did she expect?)
There's no way it didn't splatter on my shirt when it's already all over my face
I forgot I had scissors and tongs
The first time I had nokdu-pancake was at my grandma's house
It was tasty but I didn't like it because of its name
Let's put more broth in since it's little dry
Was that too much?
[The last piece]

Let's finish up
This is my second time trying buckwheat guksu
The noodles break very easily because it's 100% made of buckwheat
That doesn't mean that the texture was bad. The more I eat, the more it gets tasty
Also, buckwheat helps digestion as well
I think I'm hungry now lol
